title: Distributed Governance Research: A Functional Architecture for Selective Institutional Distribution (Working Paper v1.14)
description: Complete research corpus, working paper, and code proposing a functional architecture for distributed public project governance: citizens allocate a mandate-recorded share of an existing public budget across verifiable projects under strict role separation, milestone-gated conditional disbursement, evidential contracts, and role-based reputation — distributed allocation within mandate-bound, visible, versioned, and contestable planning scopes. Its guiding measure is delivered public value per unit of budget. Includes formal mechanism-design propositions (incentive compatibility of milestone disbursement; collusion-proofness of protocolized fiscalization), a reproducible 10,000-agent simulation, and a documented adversarial review record of forty-three attack briefs across five rounds with paired defenses and accepted resolutions, propagated through the architecture corpus except the manuscript-review round's D037-D040, whose propagation is tracked. In the model's declared reference scenario — with the central selector modelled as the evidence describes it, near-blind to diffuse harm on the low-visibility long tail — coverage-routed distributed selection recovers about 98% of the model's full-information reference against the central's about 44% (a 54-point conditional contrast). These are directional, conditional contrasts of a stylized comparative-institutions model — not calibrated real-world effects and not a validated implementation; the controlling specification is research/claim-and-estimand-contract.md. The manuscript is drafts/paper.md (Spanish translation drafts/paper.es.md); the accessible companion is docs/101_CORE_V0_MINIMAL_PUBLISHABLE_MODEL.md.
notes: Single record covering the corpus, the working paper, and code. All text and research content (corpus + manuscript) is CC BY 4.0; code (scripts/) is MIT. CC BY attribution is required for text; the MIT notice must be retained for code. See LICENSE.md.
